runTitle: Local dev turbopack trace - root CSS trim follow-up
coveredSurfaces: root css + shared route compile cold path

completedImprovements.title: Trim root global-effects.css and inline unused design-system helpers
completedImprovements.note: global-effects.css was reduced from 646 lines to 185 lines by removing unused design-system utility blocks and dead animation helpers; button/badge/card/input now carry their own Tailwind styling instead of depending on root-only tokens.

pendingImprovements.title: Trace shared route compile bottleneck after root CSS trim
pendingImprovements.note: Cold /api/version improved slightly to 78s (~11.5MB trace), and timeout logs showed Next dev reported Ready in 61.8s before route compilation, down from the previous 69.3s signal. Total cold responses for / and /dashboard still completed only at 197s and 196s with large traces (~119.8MB and ~165.5MB), so the remaining bottleneck is now the shared route compile/import graph rather than the removed root CSS block.

notes: This follow-up passed npm run type-check, npm run lint, and npm run test:quick before the new trace rerun.
Cold local-dev reruns used rm -rf .next before each trace collection to isolate startup and route compile cost.
The /api/version cold rerun completed in 78s with trace size 11,515,400 bytes.
The / cold rerun completed in 197s with trace size 119,832,551 bytes.
The /dashboard cold rerun completed in 196s with trace size 165,532,331 bytes.

Repeated timeout runs against / showed the server reached Ready in 61.8s and then stalled on route compilation, so the next target should be shared import-graph trimming across landing and dashboard paths.
